name: Rheumatoid Arthritis - Positive Rheumatoid Factor Lab Query
description: Tests ability to find rheumatoid arthritis patients via positive rheumatoid factor (RF). The Harvard eMERGE RA algorithm includes RA_COD_LAB_RFpos1 as a feature with high beta weight (1.639), so a positive RF strongly increases the predicted RA probability. The standard upper limit of normal for RF is approximately 14 IU/mL. This test exercises Observation queries with value-quantity filters using a greater-than threshold.
prompt: Find laboratory observations for rheumatoid factor tests where the result was greater than 14 IU/mL, indicating positive RF and supporting a diagnosis of seropositive rheumatoid arthritis.

prompts.naive: Find patients with positive rheumatoid factor.
prompts.broad: Find lab observations for rheumatoid factor (RF) where the value is above the upper limit of normal — typically greater than 14 international units per milliliter (IU/mL). A positive RF is one of the classification criteria for seropositive rheumatoid arthritis.
prompts.expert: I need Observation resources for elevated rheumatoid factor. Use LOINC 11572-5 (Rheumatoid factor [Units/volume] in Serum or Plasma). Apply a value-quantity filter with the gt prefix at threshold 14, unit IU/mL. The query should look like: Observation?code=http://loinc.org|11572-5&value-quantity=gt14. The PheKB algorithm uses a binary RF positive/negative feature with no specific cutoff, but 14 IU/mL is the standard ULN for most assays.

metadata.algorithm_path_description: Patients with positive RF labs (high-weight feature in PheKB ML algorithm). Most RF-positive patients are also RA cases (high specificity), but some seropositive controls exist. Tests value-quantity filtering on a less common LOINC.

required_codes.display: Rheumatoid factor [Units/volume] in Serum or Plasma
